title: Remove theme toggle and enforce always-on dark styling

requirements.text: Remove the light/dark mode toggle from the UI so users cannot switch themes from within the app.

quotes: Omit the dark mode toggle. Use an overall dark styling.

acceptanceCriteria: The header no longer renders a theme toggle button/icon.
No UI control remains that toggles between light and dark modes.
The app builds successfully with no unused/failed imports related to the removed toggle.

requirements.text: Make the app render in dark styling by default on every load (independent of system theme preference and any previously stored theme), using the existing Tailwind class-based dark theme variables.

quotes: Omit the dark mode toggle. Use an overall dark styling.

acceptanceCriteria: On first load (fresh browser profile), the app renders in dark theme.
If localStorage contains theme='light' from a previous version/session, the app still renders in dark theme on load.
There is no flash of light theme before dark theme is applied (no visible FOUC).

requirements.text: Remove or retire client-side theme persistence/toggling logic that is no longer needed after enforcing always-on dark styling.

quotes: Omit the dark mode toggle. Use an overall dark styling.

acceptanceCriteria: No code path attempts to toggle the document 'dark' class at runtime based on a stored theme value.
Theme-related code is either deleted or left unused-free (no ESLint/TypeScript errors and no dead imports in the app entry/layout components).

constraints: Do not edit any files under frontend/src/components/ui (read-only).
Do not modify immutable hook files under frontend/src/hooks (including Internet Identity and actor hooks).
nonGoals: Supporting light mode or a user-selectable theme switcher.
Adding new theming features beyond enforcing a consistent dark appearance.
